steps.title: Test antenna necessity
steps.content: The researchers concluded that thermal receptors in the antennae detect light. The strongest support would show that interfering with the antennae delays or prevents the normal escape response.

steps.title: Reject weaker evidence
steps.content: Choice A shows other body parts warming but does not isolate antennae as the detection mechanism. Choice B weakens the thermal explanation by preserving the response at reduced temperatures. Choice C compares species without testing the mechanism. The answer is D.
